examples.sentence: 彼のことだから、きっと大丈夫だと思う。
examples.translation: Because it's him, I think it will certainly be fine.
examples.translationEn: Because it's him, I think it will certainly be fine.
examples.romaji: Kare no koto dakara, kitto daijoubu da to omou.

examples.sentence: この店のことだから、料理は美味しいはずです。
examples.translation: Considering it's this restaurant, the food should be delicious.
examples.translationEn: Considering it's this restaurant, the food should be delicious.
examples.romaji: Kono mise no koto dakara, ryouri wa oishii hazu desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女のことだから、もうすぐ到着するでしょう。
examples.translation: Since it's her, she will probably arrive soon.
examples.translationEn: Since it's her, she will probably arrive soon.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no koto dakara, mou sugu touchaku suru deshou.

examples.sentence: この会社のことだから、もうすぐ新製品が発表されるでしょう。
examples.translation: Given this company, a new product will probably be announced soon.
examples.translationEn: Given this company, a new product will probably be announced soon.
examples.romaji: Kono kaisha no koto dakara, mou sugu shinseihin ga happyou sareru deshou.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun の 嫌いがある
examples.sentence: 彼は魚の嫌いがあるので、寿司を食べません。
examples.translation: He has a dislike for fish, so he doesn't eat sushi.
examples.translationEn: He has a dislike for fish, so he doesn't eat sushi.
examples.romaji: Kare wa sakana no kirai ga aru node, sushi o tabemasen.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun の 嫌いがある
examples.sentence: 彼女は高いところの嫌いがあるから、山登りはしない。
examples.translation: She has a dislike for high places, so she doesn't go mountain climbing.
examples.translationEn: She has a dislike for high places, so she doesn't go mountain climbing.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa takai tokoro no kirai ga aru kara, yamanobori wa shinai.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun の 嫌いがある
examples.sentence: 私の父は人ごみの嫌いがあるので、混雑した場所には行かない。
examples.translation: My father has a dislike for crowds, so he doesn't go to crowded places.
examples.translationEn: My father has a dislike for crowds, so he doesn't go to crowded places.
examples.romaji: Watashi no chichi wa hitogomi no kirai ga aru node, konzatsu shita basho ni wa ikanai.

examples.grammarPoint: Noun の 嫌いがある
examples.sentence: 子供の頃、私は野菜の嫌いがあったが、今は好きだ。
examples.translation: When I was a child, I had a dislike for vegetables, but now I like them.
examples.translationEn: When I was a child, I had a dislike for vegetables, but now I like them.
examples.romaji: Kodomo no koro, watashi wa yasai no kirai ga atta ga, ima wa suki da.

examples.sentence: 試験の手前、遊びに行くのはやめた方がいい。
examples.translation: Just before the exam, it's better to stop going out to play.
examples.translationEn: Just before the exam, it's better to stop going out to play.
examples.romaji: Shiken no temae, asobi ni iku no wa yameta hou ga ii.

examples.sentence: 彼女と別れる手前、思い直しました。
examples.translation: Just before breaking up with her, I changed my mind.
examples.translationEn: Just before breaking up with her, I changed my mind.
examples.romaji: Kanojo to wakareru temae, omoinaoshimashita.

examples.sentence: 締め切りの手前で、やっとレポートが完成しました。
examples.translation: I finally finished the report just before the deadline.
examples.translationEn: I finally finished the report just before the deadline.
examples.romaji: Shimekiri no temae de, yatto repooto ga kansei shimashita.

examples.sentence: 選挙の手前、政治の話が多くなります。
examples.translation: Just before the election, there will be a lot of political talk.
examples.translationEn: Just before the election, there will be a lot of political talk.
examples.romaji: Senkyo no temae, seiji no hanashi ga ooku narimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼の料理の極みは、誰もマネできない。
examples.translation: The pinnacle of his cooking is something nobody can imitate.
examples.translationEn: The pinnacle of his cooking is something nobody can imitate.
examples.romaji: Kare no ryouri no kiwami wa, daremo mane dekinai.

examples.sentence: 彼女の努力の極みが、この成功を作り出した。
examples.translation: The extreme of her effort has created this success.
examples.translationEn: The extreme of her effort has created this success.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no doryoku no kiwami ga, kono seikou wo tsukuridashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は音楽の極みを追求し続けている。
examples.translation: He continues to pursue the pinnacle of music.
examples.translationEn: He continues to pursue the pinnacle of music.
examples.romaji: Kare wa ongaku no kiwami o tsuikyuu shitsuzukete iru.

examples.sentence: この風景は美の極みだ。
examples.translation: This scenery is the epitome of beauty.
examples.translationEn: This scenery is the epitome of beauty.
examples.romaji: Kono fuukei wa bi no kiwami da.

examples.sentence: 彼の冗談には驚きの至りだ。
examples.translation: His jokes are the height of surprise.
examples.translationEn: His jokes are the height of surprise.
examples.romaji: Kare no joudan ni wa odoroki no itari da.

examples.sentence: 父がプロジェクトのリーダーになったのは、光栄の至りです。
examples.translation: It is the height of honor that my father became the project leader.
examples.translationEn: It is the height of honor that my father became the project leader.
examples.romaji: Chichi ga purojekuto no riidaa ni natta no wa, kouei no itari desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女の素晴らしい演技には感動の至りだった。
examples.translation: Her wonderful performance was the height of emotion.
examples.translationEn: Her wonderful performance was the height of emotion.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no subarashii engi ni wa kandou no itari datta.

examples.sentence: その失敗は恥ずかしさの至りだった。
examples.translation: That failure was the height of embarrassment.
examples.translationEn: That failure was the height of embarrassment.
examples.romaji: Sono shippai wa hazukashisa no itari datta.

examples.sentence: 彼が遅れるのはいざ知らず、彼女まで遅刻するとは意外だ。
examples.translation: Well, I can understand him being late, but even her...? That's surprising.
examples.translationEn: Well, I can understand him being late, but even her...? That's surprising.
examples.romaji: Kare ga okureru no wa iza shirazu, kanojo made chikoku suru to wa igai da.

examples.sentence: 夏はいざ知らず、冬にアイスクリームを食べるのは寒すぎませんか？
examples.translation: I can see eating ice cream in summer, but in winter…isn't it too cold?
examples.translationEn: I can see eating ice cream in summer, but in winter…isn't it too cold?
examples.romaji: Natsu wa iza shirazu, fuyu ni aisukuriimu o taberu no wa samusugimasen ka?

examples.sentence: 子供がテレビゲームに夢中になるのはいざ知らず、大人までそんなに夢中になるとは思わなかった。
examples.translation: I can see why kids get hooked on video games, but I didn't think even adults would get that absorbed.
examples.translationEn: I can see why kids get hooked on video games, but I didn't think even adults would get that absorbed.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga terebi geemu ni muchuu ni naru no wa iza shirazu, otona made sonnani muchuu ni naru to wa omowanakatta.

examples.sentence: 苦しい時はいざ知らず、平時でもそんなに努力するなんてすばらしい。
examples.translation: Well, I can understand working hard in tough times, but to do so even in normal times is impressive.
examples.translationEn: Well, I can understand working hard in tough times, but to do so even in normal times is impressive.
examples.romaji: Kurushii toki wa iza shirazu, heiji demo sonnani doryoku suru nante subarashii.

examples.sentence: 彼は走るはおろか、速く歩くことすらできない。
examples.translation: He can't even walk fast, let alone run.
examples.translationEn: He can't even walk fast, let alone run.
examples.romaji: Kare wa hashiru wa oroka, hayaku aruku koto sura dekinai.

examples.sentence: 日本語はおろか、英語さえ話しません。
examples.translation: He doesn't even speak English, let alone Japanese.
examples.translationEn: He doesn't even speak English, let alone Japanese.
examples.romaji: Nihongo wa oroka, eigo sae hanashimasen.

examples.sentence: 彼は食事はおろか、一日中何も飲まなかった。
examples.translation: He didn't even drink anything all day, let alone eat a meal.
examples.translationEn: He didn't even drink anything all day, let alone eat a meal.
examples.romaji: Kare wa shokuji wa oroka, ichinichijuu nani mo nomanakatta.

examples.sentence: このレストランは味はおろか、サービスさえ悪い。
examples.translation: Never mind the taste; even the service is bad.
examples.translationEn: Never mind the taste; even the service is bad.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran wa aji wa oroka, saabisu sae warui.

examples.sentence: 彼は英語はおろか、日本語までペラペラなんです。
examples.translation: He is not just fluent in English, but even in Japanese.
examples.translationEn: He is not just fluent in English, but even in Japanese.
examples.romaji: Kare wa eigo wa oroka, nihongo made perapera nan desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は数学はおろか、物理まで得意なんです。
examples.translation: She is not just good at math, but even at physics.
examples.translationEn: She is not just good at math, but even at physics.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa suugaku wa oroka, butsuri made tokui nan desu.

examples.sentence: この料理は見た目はおろか、味まで最高だ。
examples.translation: This dish is not only visually appealing, but it even tastes fantastic.
examples.translationEn: This dish is not only visually appealing, but it even tastes fantastic.
examples.romaji: Kono ryouri wa mitame wa oroka, aji made saikou da.

examples.sentence: 彼は歌はおろか、ダンスまで上手だ。
examples.translation: He is not just good at singing, but even at dancing.
examples.translationEn: He is not just good at singing, but even at dancing.
examples.romaji: Kare wa uta wa oroka, dansu made umai da.

examples.sentence: 絵を描くことはおろか、字さえ書けない。
examples.translation: Needless to say drawing pictures, he can’t even write letters.
examples.translationEn: Needless to say drawing pictures, he can’t even write letters.
examples.romaji: E wo kaku koto wa oroka, ji sae kakenai.

examples.sentence: 彼は英語はおろか、日本語もわからない。
examples.translation: Needless to say English, he doesn’t even understand Japanese.
examples.translationEn: Needless to say English, he doesn’t even understand Japanese.
examples.romaji: Kare wa eigo wa oroka, nihongo mo wakaranai.

examples.sentence: 彼女は数学はおろか、算数も得意ではない。
examples.translation: Needless to say mathematics, she isn’t even good at arithmetic.
examples.translationEn: Needless to say mathematics, she isn’t even good at arithmetic.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa suugaku wa oroka, sansuu mo tokui dewa nai.

examples.sentence: 彼は走ることはおろか、歩くことも難しい。
examples.translation: Needless to say running, he finds even walking difficult.
examples.translationEn: Needless to say running, he finds even walking difficult.
examples.romaji: Kare wa hashiru koto wa oroka, aruku koto mo muzukashii.

examples.sentence: 試験の結果はさておき、この学期はとても学ぶことが多かった。
examples.translation: Aside from the exam results, I learned a lot this semester.
examples.translationEn: Aside from the exam results, I learned a lot this semester.
examples.romaji: Shiken no kekka wa sateoki, kono gakki wa totemo manabu koto ga ookatta.

examples.sentence: 天候はさておき、明日のピクニックは楽しみにしています。
examples.translation: Putting aside the weather, I am looking forward to tomorrow's picnic.
examples.translationEn: Putting aside the weather, I am looking forward to tomorrow's picnic.
examples.romaji: Tenkou wa sateoki, ashita no pikunikku wa tanoshimi ni shiteimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼の性格はさておき、彼のスキルは認めざるを得ない。
examples.translation: Notwithstanding his personality, his skills are undeniable.
examples.translationEn: Notwithstanding his personality, his skills are undeniable.
examples.romaji: Kare no seikaku wa sateoki, kare no sukiru wa mitomezaru wo enai.

examples.sentence: 彼があまりにも遅いことはさておき、彼が来ると思われること自体が問題だ。
examples.translation: Aside from him being late, the issue is that he is expected to come at all.
examples.translationEn: Aside from him being late, the issue is that he is expected to come at all.
examples.romaji: Kare ga amari ni mo osoi koto wa sateoki, kare ga kuru to omowareru koto jitai ga mondai da.
